metadata.note: Resmetirom, VK2809, ALG-055009, and HSK-31679 fully verified.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Selective thyroid hormone receptor beta (THR-β) agonist. Brand name: REZDIFFRA®

clinical_trials.note: Primary efficacy trial for FDA approval

patents.note: THR-β agonist compounds, pharmaceutical compositions, and methods of treating NASH/MASH. Owned by Madrigal Pharmaceuticals. Patent search: https://patents.google.com/?q=Resmetirom+Madrigal

pubmed_papers.title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Controlled Trial of Resmetirom in NASH with Liver Fibrosis (MAESTRO-NASH)
Resmetirom for nonalcoholic fatty liver disease: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled phase 3 trial (MAESTRO-NAFLD-1)
Resmetirom (MGL-3196) for the treatment of non-alcoholic steatohepatitis: a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 2 trial

compounds.key_findings: Resmetirom (REZDIFFRA®) is the FIRST FDA-approved THR-β agonist for MASH/NASH (Mar 14, 2024). MAESTRO-NASH Phase 3 trial showed 25.9-29.9% NASH resolution vs 9.7% placebo (p<0.001), and 24.2-25.9% fibrosis improvement vs 14.2% placebo (p<0.001). Secondary endpoints: LDL-C reduction -13.6% to -16.3%, hepatic fat reduction -34.9% to -38.6%. FDA designations: Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track, Priority Review. 373+ PubMed publications. EMA approved August 2025.
compounds.notes: FDA Approved Mar 14, 2024. First treatment for NASH/MASH with liver fibrosis (F2-F3). Brand name: REZDIFFRA®. 80mg and 100mg oral tablets, once daily. Most common AEs: diarrhea (23-33%), nausea (15-18%).

chemical_identifiers.notes: No public data found. Kanghong primarily known for ophthalmology drugs (conbercept/Lumitin), not NASH therapies.

compounds.key_findings: No public data found for KH-629. Comprehensive search of PubChem, ClinicalTrials.gov, PubMed, and Google Patents returned no results. Kanghong Pharmaceutical is primarily known for ophthalmology drugs (anti-VEGF for AMD/DME/PCV), not NASH/MASH therapies. Compound may be very early preclinical, internal code name, or confidential development.
compounds.notes: No public data. Kanghong not known for NASH pipeline.

chemical_identifiers.notes: No public data available. Compound may be in early discovery or known by alternative code name.

compounds.key_findings: No public data found for HP-515. Comprehensive search of PubChem, ClinicalTrials.gov, PubMed, Google Patents, and company website returned no results. Likely very early-stage compound with limited public disclosure.
compounds.notes: No public data. Search recommended: Chinese regulatory databases (NMPA/CDE), CNIPA patents, recent Chinese pharma news.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Liver-selective TRβ agonist prodrug (HepDirect technology). Also known as MB07811.

patents.note: HepDirect prodrug technology patents. Key patents: Erion MD et al. PNAS 2007;104(39):15490-15495 (PMID: 17878314). Viking Therapeutics holds patents on TRβ agonists and liver-targeting thyromimetics.

pubmed_papers.title: A Liver-Specific Thyromimetic, VK2809, Decreases Hepatosteatosis in Glycogen Storage Disease Type Ia
Targeting Thyroid Hormone Receptor-Beta Agonists to the Liver Reduces Cholesterol and Triglycerides
Development and validation for bioanalysis of VK2809

compounds.key_findings: VK2809 is a liver-selective TRβ agonist prodrug (HepDirect technology) from Viking Therapeutics. Phase 2b VOYAGE trial showed 38-55% hepatic fat reduction (12 weeks), 63-75% NASH resolution vs 29% placebo (52 weeks), 44-57% fibrosis improvement vs 34% placebo. LDL-C reduction 11-20% placebo-adjusted. 94% of AEs mild/moderate, excellent GI tolerability. Mechanism: liver-targeted prodrug cleaved by CYP450 to active TRβ agonist (MB07344). 11 PubMed publications.
compounds.notes: Phase 2b completed. VOYAGE trial (NCT04173065). Also known as MB07811. HepDirect prodrug technology minimizes cardiac/thyroid effects.

patents.note: Specific ALG-055009 patent numbers not verified in public searches.

pubmed_papers.title: Discovery and Preclinical Profile of ALG-055009, a Potent and Selective Thyroid Hormone Receptor Beta (THR-β) Agonist for the Treatment of MASH

A multipart phase 1 study of the safety, pharmacodynamics and pharmacokinetics of ALG-055009, a novel thyroid hormone receptor beta (THR-β) agonist for metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH), in healthy participants

compounds.key_findings: ALG-055009 is a selective THR-β agonist from Aligos Therapeutics. Phase 1 completed (Clin Pharmacol Drug Dev 2026). Dose-dependent decreases in ApoB and LDL-C observed. Increases in SHBG. Half-life ~20 hours, rapid absorption, low variability. Well tolerated with mostly mild/moderate AEs. NO hepatic fat/NASH/fibrosis data yet (Phase 2 not started). 2 PubMed publications (2024, 2026).
compounds.notes: Phase 1 completed. Phase 2 not yet started. Lipid lowering (ApoB, LDL-C) and SHBG increase verified. No histological efficacy data available yet.

compounds.key_findings: ASC-41 is a THR-β agonist from Ascletis Pharma (1672.HK). Reported Phase 2a for NASH/MASH. Specific efficacy data, trial numbers, and publications not accessible due to website restrictions (404 errors, Cloudflare blocks). Company focuses on metabolic diseases with AI-assisted drug discovery platform.
compounds.notes: Phase 2a reported but not verified. Access restrictions on company website and clinical trial databases.

clinical_trials.note: 12-week efficacy trial in Asian patients with MASLD

patents.note: Haisco Pharmaceutical has filed patents for THR-β agonists. Specific HSK-31679 patent numbers not verified in public searches.

pubmed_papers.title: Efficacy and Safety of HSK31679 in Asian Patients With MASLD: A Randomized Controlled Trial

compounds.key_findings: HSK-31679 is a THR-β agonist from Haisco Pharmaceutical. Phase 2a trial (NCT05795517) showed significant hepatic fat reduction: -14.0% (40mg), -22.7% (80mg), -29.2% (160mg) vs -4.1% placebo (12 weeks). 80mg and 160mg doses statistically significant (p=0.002 and p<0.001). Well tolerated with diarrhea as most common TEAE (26-38% vs 4.8% placebo). No drug-related SAEs. 1 PubMed publication (Clin Gastroenterol Hepatol 2026).
compounds.notes: Phase 2a completed. NCT05795517 verified. Hepatic fat reduction -22.7% to -29.2% at 80-160mg doses. Well tolerated.
